blurb:
Not a restaurant, but worth knowing about if you're looking for a night out close to home. Cinemark Lewisville has stadium seating, self-service kiosks to skip the line, and an XD screen if you want the full big-sound experience. It's a solid option when you want to get out of the house without making a whole production of it.

With over 2,600 reviews and a 4.3 rating, most people seem pretty happy with it. Good for date nights, family outings, or just a quiet weeknight when everyone needs a break.
address: 2401 S Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA

blurb:
Regal Buffet is one of those spots that works great when you have picky eaters or a big group where nobody can agree. It's a large, roomy Chinese buffet with a wide spread that includes both classic Chinese dishes and sushi rolls. At that price point, it's hard to beat for feeding a family without a lot of fuss.

Good for a casual lunch or a no-stress dinner when you just want a lot of options. Open every day until at least 9pm, with later hours on Friday and Saturday.
address: 251 N Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
BJ's is one of those places that works for just about any group because the menu is huge and the vibe is relaxed. It's a brewhouse chain, so there's a solid lineup of house-brewed beers alongside the usual pub food. Good for a casual dinner with friends, a post-game hangout, or a family night where everyone has different opinions on what they want to eat.

The pub fare is familiar and reliable. If you're coming for drinks, the house brews are worth trying over a standard domestic. Open until midnight most nights and 1am on weekends, so it works late when a lot of places around here are already closed.
address: 2609 S Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
Rosa's is the kind of Tex-Mex spot you end up at more often than you plan to because it's fast, affordable, and the food is genuinely good. They make their tortillas in-house, which you can actually watch in some locations, and it makes a difference. Good for a quick family lunch, a breakfast run, or anytime you want solid Mexican food without spending much.

They open at 6:30am every day, which makes it a decent breakfast stop too. Prices are on the lower end, so it's easy to feed a group without thinking too hard about it.
address: 705 N Denton Tap Rd, Coppell, TX 75019, USA
hours: Monday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Wu Wei Din Chinese Cuisine - Lewisville
blurb:
This one has built up a real following, and once you try it you'll understand why. Reviewers keep coming back and calling it a favorite, which says a lot for a Chinese spot in a strip mall. The orange chicken gets mentioned a lot and people say it's the best they've had. Not generic takeout quality at all.

The pan fried potstickers are worth ordering, and the lo mein and chicken udon noodles have both gotten good marks from regulars. Good for a weeknight dinner or takeout when you want something a step above the usual. Just know they close between lunch and dinner (3pm to 4pm) every day, so plan accordingly.
address: 2505 S Stemmons Fwy #3480, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks on Roswell Road is a solid go-to when you want Chinese food that goes a little beyond the usual takeout options. The menu is eclectic, and they do things like hot pot, lobster in black bean sauce, and BBQ duck, which you don't see at every Chinese spot in the area. It's a casual place, good for a relaxed family dinner or a weeknight meal when you want something different.

The BBQ duck and the lobster in black bean sauce are the standout dishes worth trying if you want to get a feel for what makes this place a cut above the standard order. Hours run through 10pm most nights, so it works for a later dinner too.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Brooklyn Cafe has been a Sandy Springs staple for a reason. It's got a warm, comfortable feel with patio seating, a solid wine list, and the kind of menu where most people find something they like. Good for a lunch out, a weeknight dinner, or a proper sit-down meal with family. The bistro-style American food is well executed and not overpriced for what you get.

The menu covers a lot of ground in the best way, and the wine list gets consistent praise. It's the kind of place you can bring someone visiting from out of town and feel good about the choice.

Just note they are closed on Sundays, so plan accordingly.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Hearth Pizza Tavern is right next door to Canton Cooks on Roswell Road, and it's a great option when you want pizza that's a step above the delivery chain stuff. The space has exposed brick and a patio, and it feels like a comfortable neighborhood spot without being too loud or too fancy. Good for date night or a laid-back dinner with friends.

The pies here are inventive, which is a nice change of pace if you're tired of the same old toppings. They've got beer and wine to go with it, and the appetizers are worth ordering while you wait. Open until 10pm most nights, with Sunday closing at 9pm.
address: 5992 Roswell Rd NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
If you haven't been to Rreal Tacos yet, put it on your list. The vibe is lively and fun, and it's the kind of place that works equally well for a casual weeknight dinner or a late night out with friends. They're open until midnight most nights, which is a nice bonus around here.

People rave about the Esquite to start, and the margarita pitchers are a big deal at this spot. The Pineapple Guava and Passion Fruit pitchers come up constantly in reviews, and they're apparently well worth it for a group. The food and drinks together make it easy to linger.

Fair warning: it gets busy, especially on weekends. But with nearly 9,000 reviews and a 4.9 rating, it's clearly doing something right.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
Thrill Korean Steak and Bar is the kind of place that becomes a go-to once you try it. It's a big, two-level space on Roswell Road with a real energy to it, and the all-you-can-eat Korean BBQ is the main draw. You cook the meat at your table, which makes it a fun night out for a group, a date, or really anyone who likes good food and a lively atmosphere.

The Korean BBQ is the thing to order here, and the handcrafted cocktails get solid reviews too. On weekends they're open until 2am, so it also works as a late-night spot after other plans.

Dinner only Monday through Thursday (5pm), but they open at 11am on Fridays, Saturdays, and Sundays.
address: 5958 Roswell Rd, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
The Select has an Art Deco look and a menu built around modern European dishes, with an open kitchen and a conservatory dining area that makes the whole place feel a little more interesting than your average Sandy Springs dinner spot. It's a good choice when you want something a step up from casual without going full special-occasion splurge.

This is a dinner-only spot most nights, but Sunday opens up for brunch starting at 10am. Friday and Saturday they stay open until midnight, so it's also a solid option for a later dinner before or after something else.

Pricing is on the higher side, so this works better for a date night or a meal with people you actually want to linger with.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Suite 200, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM, Friday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
Nearly a perfect rating across over 5,000 reviews. That's not an accident. Mister O1 does traditional Italian thin-crust pizza, and they take it seriously. The ingredients are either locally sourced or imported from Italy, and you can taste the difference.

This is a solid pick for a casual dinner with the family or a laid-back date night. The thin crust style means it's lighter than your typical American pizza, so ordering a couple of different pies to share is a good move. They also do salads and antipasti if you want to start with something before the pizza arrives.

Open every day starting at 11:30am, with later hours on Friday and Saturday nights.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
Rreal Tacos has over 9,000 reviews and a 4.9 rating, which tells you pretty much everything you need to know. The vibe is lively and the place has energy, which makes it a great spot for a group dinner or a fun night out with friends.

Start with the Esquite and order drinks from the pitcher menu. The Pineapple Guava and the Passion Fruit pitchers both come up a lot in reviews as crowd favorites. Good balance of flavor without being too sweet or too boozy.

They're open until midnight most nights, which is handy if you're eating late. It's the kind of place that works equally well for a casual weeknight dinner or a Saturday night out.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
If you haven't tried Persian food yet, Yalda is a great place to start. It's a genuinely fun dinner spot, the kind of place that works well for a girls' night out or a group celebration. The atmosphere is warm and the service gets high marks pretty consistently.

The hummus and wings are solid starters, and the chicken shish kabob with rice with lentils and raisins is the dish reviewers keep coming back to. Save room for the tiramisu at the end. The cocktail menu is also worth exploring.

Note that there's a break between lunch and dinner service most days, so plan around that if you're going on a weeknight.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
If you're looking for a spot that feels genuinely special without being stuffy, Canoe is it. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River, and the garden setting makes it one of the prettiest places to eat in the whole Atlanta area. This is the kind of place you take out-of-town guests or your spouse for a birthday dinner and you look like a hero.

The menu is farm-to-table New American, so the ingredients are fresh and the dishes change with the seasons. It's not a cheap night out, but the food and setting justify the price for a real occasion dinner.

Just know lunch runs Monday through Friday only, and Saturday is dinner only. Sunday brunch is worth knowing about too, 10:30am to 2:30pm. Make a reservation. You'll want one.
address: 4199 Paces Ferry Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA
